label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking how the researchers found that the dogs understood the woman's intention. The options are A to D. Looking back at the text, there's a part where they mention the method the researchers used. The key sentence is: "By following the eye movements of dogs, we were able to get a first-hand look at how their minds are actually working," said Jozsef, the senior researcher. So that directly points to eye movements being the method. The study had the dogs watch a movie where a woman looked at them and then at a flowerpot. When she made eye contact and spoke directly, the dogs followed her gaze to the flowerpot. The researchers observed this behavior through their eye movements. Option B says "By reading their eye movements," which matches the quote from the researcher. The other options don't fit as well. A is about speaking directly, which was part of the experiment setup but not the method of finding out. C is facial expressions, but the text specifically mentions eye movements. D
